title: GET /api/tenant — public tenant identity for the SPA
problem: The SPA needs to know WHO the current host is — the tenant's display name, theme tokens, owner email, and whether the signed-in viewer is an operator. Resolving by Host happens centrally in _middleware.ts; this endpoint just returns that result as a JSON payload the SPA can read on mount. One endpoint serves both the marc-portal canonical host and any future white-labelled tenant.
analysis: GET only. ctx.data.tenant is attached by the middleware; this handler returns a public projection (TenantPublic) — id, display name, theme JSON, owner_email (for sign-in UX), operator role determination. If the request reached this handler, the tenant exists (middleware would have 404'd unknown hosts already). For the legacy pre-migration window, ctx.data.tenant may be null; the handler returns a default 'unknown tenant' shape that the SPA can render gracefully.

decisions.rationale: The SPA needs the public-facing fields. Anything secret (Stripe keys, internal flags) lives only in env or in tenant rows the SPA never sees. TenantPublic is the contract; new fields earn their place via this projection only.
decisions.recommendation: When adding tenant fields, default them to private. Surface only if the SPA genuinely needs them.

decisions.decision: Return a default shape when middleware passed through tenantless
decisions.rationale: Pre-migration safety net. The middleware lets requests flow through if the tenant_domains table doesn't exist yet; this handler's response stays usable instead of 500'ing. Once 0002 is universally applied this branch is dead but harmless.
decisions.recommendation: Don't remove the null-branch. It's the safety net for the same reason the middleware's branch exists.

successCriteria:
- The SPA can fetch tenant identity on mount in one request
- Unknown hosts never reach this handler (middleware 404s first)
- Pre-migration tenantless requests get a usable default shape
- TenantPublic projection never carries secrets
- A signed-in operator gets their role flag set in the response
implementation: functions/api/tenant.ts — single onRequestGet. Reads ctx.data.tenant; projects to TenantPublic. Determines operator role from email match + ADMIN_EMAILS env or tenant.owner_email match. No D1 hit here — middleware did the resolution.
userGuide: Consumed by src/lib/tenantContext.tsx on mount. To preview: GET http://localhost:8788/api/tenant — Host header determines what's returned. To add a tenant for testing locally: insert into tenant_domains.
knownLimitations: No caching at this handler; CF Pages edge caching handles it. SPA also caches via useTenant() context
TenantPublic shape evolves carefully — any consumer in the SPA that uses a field must check for its presence (TS optional) to handle older deploys

statusHistory.reason: Code-first doc co-located. Public projection contract stable.
